genre_refs: New York Post front-page styling; torn-paper collage
description: A designed social-media graphic styled as a torn-newsprint collage. The off-white top portion has a ragged torn-paper bottom edge and carries the New York Post masthead (its distinctive italic logo) next to a small waving American flag, a red 'POLITICS' tag and black 'EXCLUSIVE' tag, and a bold black headline: 'ICE rounds up over 1,000 illegal immigrants during two-week operation to honor Laken Riley.' Below the torn edge is a color photo of a smiling young brown-haired woman (Laken Riley) in a strapless black top, outdoors in front of pastel houses and palm trees, her image cut out and composited so she appears to emerge from beneath the torn headline. The composition borrows third-party news credibility (NY Post branding) and a sympathetic victim portrait to frame a mass-arrest operation as honoring a murder victim. Technique: appeal to emotion plus borrowed-authority headline. Target audience: the public and ICE supporters, presenting enforcement results as justice for an American victim of 'illegal alien crime.'
notable_text: NEW YORK POST; POLITICS / EXCLUSIVE; ICE rounds up over 1,000 illegal immigrants during two-week operation to honor Laken Riley
